free_text: Fission in the reaction of 12C with 209Bi at 73.4 and
and 84.2 MeV
Old name = S.S.Rattan, New name = Sarbjit Singh
Pelletron facility
Targets of super pure 209Bi (0.84 mg/cm2 thick) were
prepared by vacuum evaporation on 25.4 mumeter super
pure Al foil.
In order to avoid the cross contamination of fission
products due to large activity of direct reaction
products, the 209Bi target was covered with a
0.76 mumeter super pure Al foil. This will allow the
fission products to pass and will prevent the reaction
products. The whole assembly was put in a target holder
and irradiated for suitable length of time with 77 MeV
and 87.5 MeV 12C particle beam of ~100 nano-ampere.
The incident 12C energy on the Bi targets were 73.4 MeV
and 84.2 MeV. Bi targets and Al catchers were separated
from each other carefully to avoid cross contamination.
Samples were irradiated for a suitable
length of time so as to determine both the short and
long lived fission products.
In each irradiation the decay of the gamma ray
peak of the fission products were followed for
suitable length of time so as to have minimum error
due to counting statistics.
The cumulative production cross section was determined
for most of the mass chains. In case of shielded nuclei
independent production cross section was determined.
Fission product yield studies in the reaction of
73.4 and 84.2 MeV 12C with 209Bi have been carried out
using gamma ray spectrometry. The cross sections for
production of fission products have been determined.
The yield distribution of fission products was found to
be symmetric and broad with FWHM around 25 mass units
and peak near mass 107.7 and 107.0.
The average number of neutrons emitted per fission
have been found to be around 5.5 at 73.4 MeV and 7.1
at 84.2 MeV respectively
The errors listed are the standard deviations
between the results from five different irradiations
and various gamma rays.
These errors include the systematic errors added
quadratically due to:
detector efficiency (up to 5%),
fluctuation in the beam current (up to 5%),
error in the target thickness (up to 5%),
collection efficiency of fission
fragments (up to 5%) and
* gamma-ray abundances
Table 1 of J.Rad.Nucl.Chem.242(1999)551

replaced
Total fission product cross section in the reaction of
12-C with 209-Bi at 73.4 and 84.2 MeV was determined by
integrating the product yields for all the mass chains
in 209Bi(12C,f) and was found to be around 473 and
814 mb for 73.4 MeV and 84.2 MeV respectively.
